name: Reno
tagline: No State Income Tax, Lake Tahoe Access, and High-Desert Adventure
description: Reno has reinvented itself from a casino town into a thriving hub for outdoor enthusiasts and tech workers. Nevada's zero state income tax is a massive draw for FIRE retirees, and Lake Tahoe is just 45 minutes away. The city offers over 300 days of sunshine, a growing food and arts scene, and access to world-class skiing, hiking, and mountain biking. Housing costs have risen but remain well below neighboring California, making Reno a compelling tax-advantaged base for the financially independent.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate – safe in most neighborhoods; standard city precautions downtown
quickFacts.climate: Semi-arid high desert with four seasons; dry summers, snowy winters, and 300+ sunny days

housing.description: 1BR apartment in the Midtown or Wells Avenue district

dining.description: Casual Midtown restaurants and tacos 2x/week; mostly home cooking

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan through Nevada Health Link

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance; car essential for Tahoe and daily life

entertainment.description: Hiking in the Sierra, Truckee River walks, free Midtown art walks; occasional casino buffet

1M.lifestyle: Housing takes over a third of the budget at around $1,250 for a one-bedroom in Midtown, and you need a car for daily life and Tahoe trips. You cook at home most nights and eat out casually a couple of times a week. The free hiking in the Sierra foothills and walks along the Truckee River keep things enjoyable. No state income tax helps, but this is a tight budget for Reno's rising costs.

housing.description: 2BR apartment in Midtown or a small house in the Southwest Reno area

dining.description: Dining out 3-4x/week in Midtown and along the Riverwalk; local breweries

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision; manageable deductibles

transportation.description: Reliable AWD vehicle for Tahoe winter drives, full coverage insurance

entertainment.description: Mt. Rose ski pass, mountain biking, Artown festival, gym membership, occasional shows

2M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom in Midtown or a small house in Southwest Reno, a ski pass at Mt. Rose, and dining out several times a week at Midtown restaurants and breweries. An AWD vehicle handles winter Tahoe drives, and weekend trips to the lake become routine. Good health insurance is covered, and zero state income tax keeps more money in your pocket compared to neighboring California.

housing.description: 3BR home in Somersett, Caughlin Ranch, or South Reno with mountain views

dining.description: Regular fine dining at The Depot, Campo, and Reno's best; wine events at local wineries

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with comprehensive coverage and low deductibles

transportation.description: Late-model SUV for mountain roads, premium insurance, snow tires

entertainment.description: Multiple ski passes, golf membership, Burning Man base camp, river rafting trips

3M.lifestyle: A three-bedroom home in Somersett or Caughlin Ranch with Sierra views, ski passes at multiple resorts, and regular dining at places like The Depot and Campo. A late-model SUV handles mountain roads year-round. Tahoe is your regular weekend destination, and the budget leaves margin for travel and savings. No state income tax saves thousands annually compared to California.

housing.description: Custom home in Arrowcreek, Montreux, or a luxury Somersett property

dining.description: Private chef for weekly dinners, fine dining in Reno and Tahoe, exclusive wine events

healthcare.description: Concierge medical practice, premium insurance, executive wellness programs

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, second vehicle, flights from RNO to major West Coast hubs

entertainment.description: Private ski club, Montreux golf membership, heli-skiing, exclusive Tahoe experiences

domesticHelp.description: Weekly deep cleaning, snow removal, landscaping, property maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Tahoe lakefront cabin rentals, quarterly travel, art collecting, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A custom home in Arrowcreek or Montreux with mountain views, a housekeeper who comes several times a week, and concierge healthcare. A private ski club membership, regular fine dining in both Reno and Tahoe, and business class flights from RNO fit the budget. Snow removal and landscaping are covered. Zero state income tax amplifies the spending power significantly.

housing.description: Trophy estate in Montreux or Arrowcreek with panoramic Sierra and valley views

dining.description: Personal chef, exclusive wine dinners, catered events, private dining experiences

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge practice, medical travel for specialists, full wellness team

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private car service, chartered flights from RNO

entertainment.description: Major arts patronage, international ski trips, private events, exclusive club memberships

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, personal assistant, groundskeeper

luxuryExtras.description: Tahoe lakefront property, first-class international travel, art collection, major philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A large estate in Montreux or Arrowcreek with panoramic Sierra views, a lakefront property near Tahoe, full-time domestic help including a housekeeper and property manager, and a cook who comes in several times a week. First-class travel, concierge healthcare, and fine dining whenever you like are all comfortable. Zero state income tax on every dollar withdrawn makes this one of the more tax-efficient places to hold a large portfolio.
